REENTRY_EVIDENCE_MAPPING.title: Logical Overrides Properly Trigger Canonical Re-Entry Following Profitable Exit

observations.title: Logical Overrides Properly Trigger Canonical Re-Entry Following Profitable Exit
observations.summary: The simulator properly authorized a new BUY_PUT position at 10:11 AM, filled at $0.28. Internal diagnostics verify that logical overrides correctly cleared previous re-entry blockers. Specifically, 'cleared prior attempt extreme' and 'fresh local/session frontier' were both logically present following the previous profitable exit, allowing the canonical signal to execute accurately as physical price action confirmed aggressive bearish flow.

observations.title: AI Narrative Detail Confirms New Evidence, structured Diagnostics Fail to Trigger Reentry Override
observations.summary: The reentry block persists despite the AI journal detailing material new evidence, indicating a failure to map prose narrative into structured diagnostics.

observations.title: Structured Diagnostics Fail to Recognize Journaled Momentum Evidence for PUT Reentry Authorization
observations.summary: The dynamic re-entry block persists despite the AI journal explicitly confirming material new evidence. Specifically, the narrative details a "violent break of stasis" and that local leads and price are "synced negatively to the structural bearish mandate," creating a "timing window active." The structured diagnostics fail to recognize this evidence, preventing the logical override for the GEX_VELOCITY blocker.

observations.title: Persistent AI Diagnostic Failure Blocks Authorization of Dynamic PUT Reentry Despite Intense Momentum Narrative
observations.summary: The dynamic re-entry block persists despite repeated, explicit AI journal entries detailing material new evidence. The narrative confirms a "violent resumption of momentum," price "piercing below 745.0," and that price and internal leads are "synced negatively." Crucial structured diagnostics ('frontierThreshold', 'freshFrontier') remain false, failing to map this prose into the logical override required to clear the GEX_VELOCITY block, reproducing the failure seen at tick 249.

observations.title: AI Set Arbitrary 'Sluggishness' Threshold, Blocking Valid PUT Reentry Despite Recognized Downside Impulse
observations.summary: The dynamic re-entry block persists despite the AI journal at 2:30 PM detailing a break of stasis to 744.35 and "accel with downside slope." Struct Diagnostics ('clearPriorAttemptExtreme') remain false, failing to map this impulse. Contradictorily, the journal simultaneously calls the move "sluggish" because it didn't "shatter 744.0," setting an arbitrary threshold that overrides playbook criteria and reproduces the failure to authorize reentry seen at ticks 249 and 269.

observations.title: Valid Re-entry Executed; Diagnostics Correctly Apply Logic Override
observations.summary: The system successfully executed a canonical re-entry BUY_CALL at 10:11 AM. Re-entry diagnostics correctly recognized 'material new evidence' (new extreme, fresh frontier, cleared prior extreme), logically overriding the dynamic GEX_VELOCITY block from the prior loss. This verifies the re-entry logic from prior build regressions is functioning correctly in this scenario.

observations.title: Reentry Diagnostics Fail to Recognize Cleared extremes with Price Objective Surplus
observations.summary: Reentry is blocked because diagnostics claim "clearedPriorAttempt" and "freshFrontier" are false. However, current SPY price (749.65) objectively exceeds the prior attempt entry spot (749.50) and previous local high (749.55). This indicates a repeated regression where logical override criteria are incorrectly false despite supportive market data, reproducing failures seen in previous builds despite earlier verification of fix at tick 43. Note drawback block is correctly active.

observations.title: Re-entry Logic Override Fix Verified
observations.summary: The system successfully executed a canonical re-entry BUY_CALL at 9:57 AM (tick 28). Re-entry diagnostics correctly mapped market data to recognize 'cleared prior attempt extreme,' overriding the dynamic block from the prior loss at tick 9. This verifies the fix for reentry logic regressions where logical override criteria were identified but not applied.

observations.title: AI Narrative Describes High Conviction Logic Override, Diagnostics Fails to Update Reentry State
observations.summary: The AI journal explicitly details material new evidence and a 'high-conviction setup forming' for a BUY_CALL, yet the structured reentry diagnostics remain blocked by 'repeated GEX_VELOCITY' with no stated evidence. This indicates a critical breakdown and regression where the model fails to apply its logical override criteria based on recognized narrative, repeating failures reported in previous build sequences.

observations.title: Fix Verified Stability: Reentry Diagnostics Dynamically Map Price Extension to Override Material Evidence
observations.summary: Fix Verified Stability: Reentry logic override continues to perform stably at tick 301. Internal diagnostics correctly map immediate market movement ("Price ticked up to 7542") to recognized material new evidence ("cleared prior attempt extreme" and "new extreme beyond prior episode"), maintaining re-entry 'allowed' status while properly respecting the dynamic drawdown review blocker. This confirms sustained stabilization of logic mapping.

observations.title: Persistence of Invalid Exit-Execution Intent Following Vehicle Failure Exit

observations.title: Persistent Open-Position Logic Following Vehicle Failure

observations.title: Post-Loss State Reset Verified Stable
observations.summary: Following a MAX_LOSS_LIMIT trade exit at tick 20 (9:49 AM), the intent engine correctly transitioned to WAIT mode with zero readiness at tick 21. The applied blocker 'POSITION_CLOSED_MAX_LOSS_LIMIT' is functionally correct. This verifies stable state management immediately following a forced loss exit.

reviews.conclusion:
### Confirmed Facts
At marketTime 9:49 AM (Tick 20 derived), the trader experienced a MAX_LOSS_LIMIT exit on a 756C position, realizing a -16.0% PnL. Contemporaneous data for 9:50 AM (Tick 21) shows the intent engine successfully cleared and reset its state: intent to WAIT, zero readiness/confidence, and a applied blocker: 'POSITION_CLOSED_MAX_LOSS_LIMIT'. Journal entries directly mention: "POST_EXIT_STATE_RESET... position, intent, pending decision, and queued cognition cleared atomically."

### Interpretation
The primary observation in finding QA-1783888440158 is verified. Immediately following the forced loss exit, the automated system demonstrated stable state management by atomically resetting operational parameters and applying an immediate global block.

### Uncertainty and Questions
The durability of the specific 'POSITION_CLOSED_MAX_LOSS_LIMIT' blocker was short. By Tick 22 (9:51 AM), the system state attempted to transition to PREPARE_CALL but was prevented by 'REENTRY_REASSESS\_REQUIRED'.

While this demonstrates robust hierarchical logic (global reset block followed by episode constraints), the session snapshots do not provide the explicit code specification governing this transition priority. We accept the observed behavior as documented. Continuing monitoring of re-entry stability is recommended regarding the distinction between a fresh divergence signal and the failed momentum thesis.

observations.title: Valid Post-Loss Blocker Override as Model Resumes CALL Intent
observations.summary: Stable transition and disciplinary logic override are validated at tick 271. Following the brief logical switch to PUT and subsequent disengagement, the intent engine returned to PREPARE_CALL. Model diagnostics correctly recognized 'new extreme beyond prior episode' and 'cleared prior attempt extreme' as materiel new evidence to override the stale 'No genuinely new evidence' lock from the 12:36 PM loss. Entry is currently appropriately blocked by active playbook gaps for Price persistence and Acceleration.

reviews.conclusion:
### Confirmed Facts
At 12:36 PM (Tick 187), the system logged a max loss exit on a CALL contract (755C).
Between Ticks 269 (1:58 PM) and 274 (2:03 PM), analysis shows the Market Brain consistently maintained a CALL direction (Confidence 60-70%).
During this window, the procedural discipline lock from the 12:36 PM loss was actively evaluated and removed. Re-entry diagnostics explicitly cite 'new extreme beyond prior episode' and 'cleared prior attempt extreme' as the new evidence condition for `discipline.allowed: true` with `override: null`.
Execution remained blocked at Tick 271, as readiness was 69, below the 80 threshold due to persistent tactical gaps: 'Current leg agrees', 'Price persistence', and 'Acceleration active'.

### Interpretation
The post-loss procedural lock functioned correctly upon initialization. The subsequent disengagement by the discipline logic displays the model's capacity to recognize renewed structural evidence and supersede simple logical cooldowns, which is valid disciplinary logic. Crucially, tactical playbook gaps appropriately provided a second layer of defense, preventing immediate execution on the removal of the disciplinary hold and ensuring re-entry requires tactical confirmation.

### Uncertainty and Questions
1. Contradiction in Diagnostics: The re-entry diagnostics at Tick 273 (diagnostics.reentry) show `clearedPriorAttempt: true` yet `freshFrontier: false`. The model reasoning for overriding based on new extremes appears logically inconsistent with the metric indicating no fresh frontier was established.
2. Unclear Definitions: Operational thresholds for 'new extreme' and 'cleared prior attempt' are missing. Without specified parameters, auditing the logical rigor of the override is impossible.
3. Data Stability: While DATA_STATE was 'HEALTHY', TRANSMISSION_STATE concurrently registered 'DELAYED' with failed ticks in multiple observations (270, 272, 273). The integrity of market data during the override determination window requires verification.

### Check Required
A code inspection or detailed human review of the discipline definition is necessary to resolve the contradiction between the criteria and the metrics (new extreme vs. freshFrontier: false).
questions: Clarify why re-entry diagnostics show freshFrontier: false while simultaneously validating the override condition based on new extremes.
Define the quantifiable parameters defining 'new extreme' and 'cleared prior attempt'.
Explain how data status can remain HEALTHY despite escalating 'failedTicks' and a 'TRANSMISSION_DELAYED' state.
claimsToVerify: Criteria for 'new extreme beyond prior episode' and 'cleared prior attempt extreme'
Impact of TRANSMISSION_DELAYED state on decision integrity
Consistency of diagnostics showing freshFrontier: false alongside validated override conditions
